items.headline: Iran warns of readiness for war and economic costs as US talks falter
items.summary: Iranian leaders and TV have ramped up pressure on the US with a stream of messaging to domestic and foreign audiences.

items.headline: Middle East updates: Big crowds at Nakba Day rally in London
items.summary: Big crowds have gathered in London to honor Nakba Day. Meanwhile, Iran's state TV reports that European countries are discussing the safe transit of ships through the Strait of Hormuz. Nakba means 'catastrophe' in Arabic and refers to the mass displacement an…

items.headline: Israel, Lebanon say extending ceasefire despite new strikes
items.summary: Israel and Lebanon agreed to extend a ceasefire and hold expanded talks on a political settlement, the United States announced Friday, even as Israel carried out new strikes that it insists are not subject to the truce.

items.headline: Netanyahu’s Office Reveals Israeli PM Secretly Visited UAE During Iran Conflict
items.summary: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu made an undeclared trip to the United Arab Emirates (UAE) at the height of the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran, his office said on Wednesday, May 13, according to an RT report [1]. The visit, which his office said resulted i…

items.headline: Vietnam-Bound Supertanker Halted by US Navy Resumes Journey
items.summary: A Vietnam-bound supertanker carrying 2 million barrels of Iraqi crude, which idled for days in the Gulf of Oman after it was halted by US forces, has resumed its journey.

items.headline: House vote on Iran war powers resolution fails for third time
items.summary: The vote on a Democratic resolution to rein in Mr. Trump's authority to strike Iran was 212-212, falling just short of a majority.

items.headline: Why Congress Can’t Stop Trump’s Iran War
items.summary: A half-century of messy politics has inverted the Constitution’s design—and there’s no easy fix.

items.headline: Three Senate Republicans break with Trump on Iran war powers vote
items.summary: Senate Republicans fractured on Iran war powers as Rand Paul and GOP colleagues voted to end Trump's Operation Epic Fury military campaign.

"The War Powers Resolution is pretty clear here. It requires the president to terminate hostilities within 60 days absent congressional authorization."

items.headline: Trump doesn't need Congress to restart Iran strikes: Hegseth
items.summary: The Iran war, now well into its third month, has caused oil prices to soar globally as Iran continues to lock down the Strait of Hormuz.

items.headline: Chinese undersea device found near Bali may be part of something bigger
items.summary: The discovery of a Chinese undersea monitoring device in a strategic Indonesian waterway last month "shows the extent of both China's reach and its undersea ambitions" and has implications for Australia's defence strategy, say experts.

items.headline: Iran War: Trump in China, Ship Seized in the Strait, War Powers Vote Comes This Close
items.summary: Trump was in China, but that didn't stop Iran from seizing and sinking ships, and a War Powers vote almost passed.

items.headline: Amid Trump-Xi Meeting, US Coalition Urges Congress to ‘Prioritize Peace’ and End ‘Confrontational Posture’ With China

items.headline: America holds the advantage as Trump meets Xi in high-stakes summit
items.summary: President Donald Trump meets Chinese President Xi Jinping in Beijing backed by military and trade leverage built over 16 months of geopolitical maneuvering.

items.headline: Oil markets could be a month away from the moment of truth. Brace for a ‘non-linear’ price spike and panic buying, analysts warn
items.summary: Oil prices could top $130-$140 a barrel next month if the strait remains closed and inventory depletion rates remain steady, according to Capital Economics.

items.summary: US stock market today suffered a sharp selloff as the Dow Jones, S&P 500, and Nasdaq crashed after President Donald Trump ended his China visit without easing deeper market fears. The S&P 500 fell 0.86% to 7,436, the Nasdaq dropped 1.25%, and the Dow lost 366…

items.headline: Stock market today: Dow, S&P 500, Nasdaq sink as yields jump amid inflation jitters
items.summary: Inflation worries are preying on investors as they weigh the outcomes of the Trump-Xi summit in China.

items.headline: Polestar says ‘pump anxiety’ has replaced range anxiety. Its balance sheet tells a more complicated story.

items.headline: A proper Trump-Xi summit would deal with nukes
items.summary: Nuclear weapons may not dominate discussions between US President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping this week, but analysts argue the issue is becoming increasingly urgent as China rapidly expands and modernises its atomic arsenal.

items.headline: News24 | Putin hails test-firing of nuclear-capable ‘Satan II’ intercontinental ballistic missile
items.summary: Russia has test-fired a new intercontinental ballistic missile, with President Vladimir Putin describing the nuclear-capable Sarmat weapon as the world’s “most powerful” missile.

items.headline: Hezbollah: Qassam commander's martyrdom shows Israel respects no agreement
items.summary: Hezbollah strongly condemns the assassination of a senior Hamas commander in an Israeli strike on the Gaza Strip, describing the attack as a violation of a standing ceasefire and denouncing the Israeli regime for disregarding negotiated commitments.

items.headline: Ukrainian president Volodymyr Zelensky visits site of Kyiv apartment strike as death toll reaches 24
items.summary: Ukrainian president Volodymyr Zelensky called on Friday for Moscow to be punished after laying red roses at the rubble of a Kyiv apartment building where a Russian missile strike killed 24 people, including three children.

items.headline: Israel’s Long War on Lebanon : A Joint History of the Zionist and Lebanese Entities
items.summary: Reviewing a century of colonial violence in Palestine and Lebanon illuminates the current Israeli occupation and what it would take to resist it.
